- After write operations (create, update, delete), resources may take up to 30 seconds to propagate.
- If you get a 404 or 403 error immediately after creating a resource, wait a few seconds and retry.

content.text:
The ReadMcpResourceTool was unable to find the resource "mittwald://user/api/token/get". This indicates that the resource does not exist or is not available on the Mittwald MCP server.

To test the mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_api_token_get tool, I will need to directly call the tool using the provided parameters instead of looking it up through the MCP resources.

The mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_api_token_get tool returned an error. The error message indicates that the command was killed with a SIGTERM signal, likely due to a timeout.

This suggests that there may be an issue with the Mittwald MCP server or the API token endpoint. Further investigation would be needed to determine the root cause of the failure.

Since the tool did not successfully execute, I cannot confirm whether it is working correctly. I will report this as a FAILURE.
